title: Calendar
description: Calendar renders days, months, and years views for single-date picking, range flows, and constrained scheduling surfaces with previous and next navigation.
intro: Use `ui-calendar` when users need a visible calendar surface instead of a compact field. It works for booking, review scheduling, availability picking, and range selection where the month context matters and the user benefits from seeing adjacent days.
importCode: import { CalendarComponent, type CalendarDay, type CalendarView } from 'ui';

featureSections.title: Basic single-date selection
featureSections.description: The default pattern is a single visible month where users can pick one date and move month by month.

featureSections.title: Days, months, and years views
featureSections.description: The component supports dedicated days, months, and years surfaces, so advanced date flows can jump across larger time ranges without forcing repeated next-month clicks.

featureSections.title: Sizes and month or year picker
featureSections.description: Size and header picker behavior change how prominent the control feels. Smaller variants suit dense forms, while larger ones fit more touch-friendly scheduling surfaces.

featureSections.title: Min and max constraints
featureSections.description: When only part of the month is valid, disabled dates should stay visible so users understand the available booking window instead of guessing why selection fails.

featureSections.title: Range selection with hover preview
featureSections.description: Range picking is stronger when users can see both confirmed boundaries and the provisional hover span before they commit the second date.

featureSections.title: Booking panel composition
featureSections.description: A practical calendar often lives inside a booking or review panel with availability context, summary text, and follow-up actions around the calendar itself.

accessibility.title: Current semantics
accessibility.body:
`ui-calendar` exposes interactive day, month, and year cells as buttons, but it does not currently provide a full ARIA grid pattern.

| Element | Current behavior |
| --- | --- |
| previous and next controls | native button semantics |
| month, day, and year cells | button semantics through `ui-button` |
| disabled dates | native disabled button state |

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
The component currently relies on standard button focus order rather than a roving-focus calendar grid.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | moves across the header controls and visible cells |
| `Enter` / `Space` | activates the focused day, month, or year button |
| browser focus order | determines movement through the visible buttons |

accessibility.title: Labels and announcements
accessibility.body: Because the surface is button-based, the surrounding heading or panel label should explain what the calendar is for, such as booking, scheduling, or range selection. If you need stronger screen-reader guidance, add nearby text that announces the current selection and allowed date window.

apiSections.title: CalendarComponent - ui-calendar
apiSections.lead: Standalone calendar surface for single-date and range-oriented flows. It renders separate days, months, and years views and emits navigation and selection events so parent components can own the actual date state.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Visible month context used by all views.
Transient hovered date used for preview range behavior.
Density token applied to navigation and cell buttons.
Turns the center header button into a month or year picker trigger in days view.
Optional callback used to mark provisional hover ranges before the end date is chosen.

blocks.title: Outputs and models
rows: Emits the chosen month index in months view.
Emits the chosen year in years view.
View-switch requests emitted from header interactions.
Hover events useful for previewing a range before confirmation.
Public view type used to drive the visible calendar surface.
{ date, day, isCurrentMonth, isToday, isSelected, isInRange?, isRangeStart?, isRangeEnd?, isDisabled }
Day-cell model returned by selection and hover events.

paragraphs: The component is intentionally state-light. Parent components own the current month, selected dates, range logic, and responses to navigation events.
For compact form entry, use a field-level date component instead. This calendar is stronger when the visible month context matters.
